articles.title: Chelsea captain Bright retires - what now for ex-England defender?
articles.summary: Chelsea captain Millie Bright announces she has retired from football with immediate effect because of injury.

articles.title: The Europa League winner turned director, actor, DJ and photographer
articles.summary: Alfie Whiteman picked up a Europa League winner's medal last year, now at 27 he has retired from football and is opening his first photography exhibition.

articles.title: Can Griezmann end his Atletico love story in style?
articles.summary: Antoine Griezmann's long love affair with Atletico Madrid is drawing to a close, but can the Frenchman bid farewell by securing the Champions League?

articles.summary: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the Premier League relegation battle.

articles.title: Chelsea and England defender Bright retires
articles.summary: Chelsea club captain and former England international Millie Bright has retired from football with immediate effect.

articles.title: A record-breaking semi-final - the antidote to modern football?
articles.summary: A scintillating semi-final first leg between Paris St-Germain and Bayern Munich left many to wonder if this was the antidote to modern football.

articles.title: Why Mourinho could be Real's 'ultimate wildcard'
articles.summary: With Real Madrid out of the Champions League, and falling behind in La Liga, noise around manager Alvaro Arbeloa's future is unsurprisingly increasing. Could Jose Mourinho be the answer?

articles.title: 'Tottenham messed up' - Spurs win at last, but is it too late?
articles.summary: Speaking on the Monday Night Club, football writer Mark Ogden and former Premier League striker Chris Sutton discuss how Tottenham appear to be heading towards relegation from the Premier League.

articles.title: Man City left frustrated by fixture pile-up
articles.summary: Manchester City face a fixture pile-up of four games in 11 days next month after the Premier League confirmed the rescheduled dates for two of their key matches in the race for the title.

articles.title: Rangers have more chance of title than Celtic - Sutton
articles.summary: Former Celtic striker Chris Sutton believes arch-rivals Rangers have more chance of winning the Scottish Premiership this season than the Parkhead club.

articles.title: Brighton plan Europe's first purpose-built £80m women's stadium
articles.summary: Brighton and Hove Albion release plans for Europe's first purpose-built women's football stadium, which will cost £75-80m.

articles.title: Football Daily
articles.summary: Aaron Paul & Jobi McAnuff look ahead to the final weekend of the EFL season.

articles.title: Ranking the players who could decide the Champions League semi-finals
articles.summary: Which players will prove key to their team’s chances in the Champions League last four? We've ranked the top 10.

articles.title: Arsenal defender Catley signs new contract
articles.summary: Arsenal and Australia defender Steph Catley signs a new contract with the Women's Super League club.

articles.title: 'Impact could be enormous if we get this right' | How soon could we see NRL x SL?
articles.summary: On The Bench podcast, Kris Radlinski and Karl Fitzpatrick discuss the possibility of the NRL merging with the Super League.

articles.title: Will Vipotnik be the next big Championship exit?
articles.summary: The future of Zan Vipotnik looks set to be the transfer storyline of the summer at Swansea City, with the Welsh club expecting interest in their star striker.

articles.title: Nijman wins fifth ranking title of 2026 as Greaves loses in last 16
articles.summary: Wessel Nijman maintained his astonishing start to 2026 by securing his fourth Players Championship - and fifth ranking title - of the year at the latest tournament in Milton Keynes.
